findings.title: Lacks modern tool-calling implementation standards and control flow patterns

evidence.heading: Acting / Tool Invocation
evidence.excerpt: The agent looks at its current goal and the plan it just made. It then picks the best tool, such as a web search, a database query, or a calculator. The agent fills in the needed inputs and sends the call. The external system does the heavy work and returns a result.

findings.recommendation: Update the content to explicitly cover modern tool invocation paradigms: JSON Schema-driven function calling, parallel/sequential tool execution, structured outputs, and streaming responses. Reference native API standards (OpenAI, Anthropic) and popular agent frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen) to bridge high-level concepts with current industry implementation patterns.
